source.section: Part 10 - Offence: Weapons & Armament > 3 - Air-to-Air Weapons > 3.3 - AIM-9X Sidewinder (JHMCS)

steps.action: Set IR COOL switch to NORM. Infrared seeker in the AIM-9 nose must be cooled down to increase sensitivity and reduce background noise. There is enough coolant for 3 hours.
Set HMD switch to BRT (Fully Right) to power up the JHMCS (Joint Helmet Cueing System)
Weapon Select Switch – SIDEWINDER, or « LSHIFT+S »
Master Mode switch will be automatically set to A/A

Move your head/helmet and try to place the DAC (Dynamic Aiming Cue) and AIM-9X Seeker Circle on the nearest target. Sensor Control Switch AFT/FWD/LEFT/RIGHT DAC (Dynamic Aiming Cue) Elevation Angle (deg) AIM-9X Seeker Circle AIM-9X Sidewinder selected & armed Number of missiles available The HMD (Helmet-Mounted Display) and JHMCS (Joint Helmet-Mounted Cueing System) allow the pilot to project the Heads-Up Display in his field of vision at all times. It also allows the slaving of sensors and weapons to the helmet’s line of sight. In the Hornet, the JHMCS is very useful for using missiles like the AIM-9X, an upgraded version of the AIM-9 with TVC (Thrust Vectoring Control) allowing 80 deg off-boresight shots. Weapon Select Switch FWD: Select Sparrow AFT: Select Gun PRESS DOWN: Select Sidewinder RIGHT: Select Amraam LEFT: No Function

When DAC (Dynamic Aiming Cue) and Sidewinder Seeker Circle are placed on a target, press and hold the Cage/Uncage switch to uncage the Sidewinder (« C » by default). Once uncaged, the Sidewinder should be actively looking for a lock on the closest heat signature.
Once the Sidewinder is uncaged and has locked a heat signature, the Seeker Circle will become smaller and a high-pitched audio sound confirms that the missile’s seeker has acquired a solid lock.
Squeeze the gun trigger (« Spacebar ») to launch missile.
